text:
The Process of Photosynthesis in Plants
Abstract
Photosynthesis is the process by which green plants produce food using sunlight, carbon dioxide,
and water.
This process occurs in the chloroplasts of plant cells and produces glucose and oxygen as
products.
Photosynthesis is essential for life on Earth because it provides energy for plants and releases
oxygen needed by living organisms.
Introduction
Plants require energy to grow, reproduce, and survive.

metadata.section_heading: The Process of Photosynthesis in Plants

text:
roduction
Plants require energy to grow, reproduce, and survive.
Unlike animals, plants produce their own
food through photosynthesis.
This process mainly occurs in leaves where chlorophyll absorbs sunlight and converts it into
chemical energy.
Components Required
Sunlight - provides energy.
Water - absorbed through roots.
Carbon Dioxide - enters leaves through stomata.
Chlorophyll - green pigment that captures sunlight.
Process
Light Reaction: Sunlight energy splits water molecules and releases oxygen.

metadata.section_heading: The Process of Photosynthesis in Plants

text:
ion: Sunlight energy splits water molecules and releases oxygen.
Dark Reaction: Carbon dioxide combines with hydrogen to produce glucose.
Chemical Equation
6CO2 + 6H2O + Sunlight → C6H12O6 + 6O2
Conclusion
Photosynthesis supports life by producing oxygen and forming the base of the food chain.
